meta.title: Binding of Ribonucleic Acids to the RNP-antigen Protein of Influenza Viruses

abstractHtml: Influenza virus plus and minus strand RNA complexed with RNP-antigen protein quite efficiently, while double-stranded virus RNA was not significantly bound. Sindbis and Newcastle disease virus RNA attached to a much lower degree. A small AMP-rich RNA fraction of uninfected cells also formed a complex with the virus RNP-antigen protein.
